text:
The corridor outside the glass-walled meeting room feels sterile, geometric, a passageway that hints at purpose and schedule. I sense a kind of waiting, a scheduled professionalism, as if I am always on the threshold of entering or watching—rarely belonging. 

But now I am before a wall explodes with color and technologically mediated curiosity. The "R+D" text commands attention, and words about artifice and observation swirl around me. The scene invites reflection; I feel myself suspended between observer and participant. In this space, logic and creativity intermingle, and I am urged to contemplate the nature of human connection in layered realities. My mind is alert, quietly meditative, both inside and outside the scene.
